name: Pico Lopera Loop Trail
name_original: Cerro Lopera — circular por las Sierras de Tejeda, Almijara y Alhama
description: This moderate 15.4-mile (24.8 km) mountain biking loop in Sierras de Tejeda, Almijara y Alhama offers challenging terrain and expansive view
region_intro: Mountain bike trails Albuñuelas are set within a mountainous region characterized by diverse landscapes and an extensive network of paths. The area features pine and olive forests, with elevations reaching up to 1426m, providing varied terrain for mountain biking. River valleys like the Rio Saleres, with its steep gorges and waterfalls, contribute to a verdant environment. The village itself sits at 730 meters above sea level, offering broad vistas across the surrounding countryside. Best mountain bike trails around Albuñuelas The most popular mountain bike trail is Pico Lopera Loop Trail, a 15.4 miles (24.8 km) trail that takes 2 hours 31 minutes to complete. This moderate route offers a balanced challenge with varied terrain. Another top favourite among local mountain bikers is Vereda del Collado del Fraile – BullBike MTB & E-Bike Circuit loop from Padul, a difficult 34.7 miles (55.8 km) path. This route features significant elevation changes and technical sections, ideal for experienced riders. Local mountain bikers also love the Ruins of La Escribana – Altos de Jayena loop from Padul, a 44.6 miles (71.7 km) trail leading through mountainous terrain, often completed in about 7 hours 10 minutes. This difficult route provides a long-distance challenge with rewarding views. Mountain biking around Albuñuelas is defined by mountainous terrain, pine and olive forests, and river valleys. The network offers options for different ability levels, from moderate paths to challenging, long-distance routes. The routes in Albuñuelas are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.8 stars from over 190 reviews. More than 750 mountain bikers have used komoot to explore Albuñuelas's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many mountain bike trails are there in Albuñuelas?
A: Albuñuelas offers a wide variety of mountain bike trails, with over 90 routes available. These range from easy paths suitable for beginners to challenging, long-distance routes for experienced riders.

Q: Are there any easy mountain bike trails in Albuñuelas?
A: Yes, Albuñuelas has a selection of easy mountain bike trails. While the region is known for its mountainous terrain, there are 4 routes specifically rated as easy, providing gentler options for those looking for a less strenuous ride.

Q: What kind of terrain can I expect on mountain bike trails around Albuñuelas?
A: The mountain bike trails around Albuñuelas feature diverse terrain, including pine and olive forests, river valleys, and mountainous sections. You'll encounter everything from loose, dry, fast-flowing trails to rocky sections and tight corners. Higher altitudes might offer muddier or grassy paths with good grip.

Q: Are there any circular mountain bike routes in Albuñuelas?
A: Yes, many of the mountain bike routes in Albuñuelas are circular, allowing you to start and end at the same point. An example of a popular circular route is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1017231613/pico-lopera-loop-trail" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Pico Lopera Loop Trail&lt;/a&gt;, a moderate 24.8 km ride.

Q: What are some challenging mountain bike trails for experienced riders?
A: For experienced riders seeking a challenge, Albuñuelas offers several difficult routes. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/31617844" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Vereda del Collado del Fraile – BullBike MTB & E-Bike Circuit loop from Padul&lt;/a&gt; is a difficult 55.8 km path with significant elevation changes. Another demanding option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/5288788" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Ruins of La Escribana – Altos de Jayena loop from Padul&lt;/a&gt;, a 71.7 km trail through mountainous terrain.

Q: Are there mountain bike trails with stunning views in Albuñuelas?
A: Absolutely. Albuñuelas is nestled in a mountainous region, offering spectacular panoramic views of pine and olive forests, and the surrounding countryside. The village itself sits at 730 meters above sea level, providing broad vistas. Tracks like the Camino Forrestal de Albuñuelas are known for their magnificent scenery.

Q: What natural attractions can I see along the mountain bike trails?
A: The region boasts several natural attractions. You can explore the steep gorges and waterfalls of the Rio Saleres valley, such as the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/87627" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Río Verde Waterfalls, Barranco de las Chortales&lt;/a&gt;, or the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3823223" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Barranco de la Luna Gorge&lt;/a&gt;. The area is also rich in springs and streams, contributing to a verdant landscape.

Q: Are there any historical or cultural landmarks near the mountain bike routes?
A: Yes, you can find interesting landmarks. For instance, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4060317" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Old Hermitage&lt;/a&gt; is a notable religious building in the area. The region also has abandoned mines like Mine Centenillo, offering unique exploration opportunities for those interested in local history.

Q: What do other mountain bikers enjoy most about mountain biking in Albuñuelas?
A: The mountain bike trails in Albuñuelas are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.8 stars from over 190 reviews. Riders often praise the diverse landscapes, the extensive network of well-worn and picturesque tracks, and the variety of terrain suitable for different skill levels.

Q: Can I find mountain bike trails that pass through mountain passes or viewpoints?
A: Yes, the mountainous terrain of Albuñuelas includes several mountain passes and viewpoints. You might encounter areas like &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/1002788" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Carretera de la Cabra&lt;/a&gt; or enjoy a &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/6384695" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;View of the Sierra de Tejeda Mountains&lt;/a&gt; along your ride. These spots often provide excellent photo opportunities and challenging climbs.

Q: Are there any longer mountain bike routes for endurance training?
A: For those looking for longer rides, Albuñuelas offers several routes that provide significant distance and elevation. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/14594028" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Mesón Los Prados – Cerro Lopera loop from Morro de los Prados&lt;/a&gt; is a difficult 47.5 km route, ideal for endurance training with substantial elevation gains.
tips: The summit, located at an altitude of 1,485 meters, has an Infoca surveillance post and a beautiful panoramic viewpoint.
This is a starting point for any area of the Sierra de Tejeda, Almijara and Alhama, here is the restaurant of Los Prados, a good place to eat.

faqs.question: How long does it typically take to complete the Cerro Lopera mountain biking route?
faqs.answer: The Komoot data suggests a moving time of approximately 2 hours and 30 minutes for this 24.8 km route. However, given its moderate difficulty, 642 meters of elevation gain, and potentially challenging S3-S6 technical sections, most riders should plan for a total duration of 4 to 6 hours, allowing for breaks, enjoying the views, and navigating trickier parts.

faqs.question: What is the starting point for the Cerro Lopera circular mountain bike trail, and is there parking available?
faqs.answer: While the exact trailhead isn't specified, as a circular route, you can typically start from various points. Parking information is not explicitly provided, but it's common for trails within the Sierras de Tejeda, Almijara y Alhama Natural Park to have designated parking areas near popular access points. We recommend checking local maps or recent user comments on Komoot for the most current parking details.

faqs.question: Is the Cerro Lopera mountain bike trail suitable for beginners?
faqs.answer: Komoot classifies this as a 'moderate' mountain bike ride requiring 'good fitness'. While some descriptions suggest it's 'suitable for all skill levels', the presence of technical sections rated S3 to S6 on the Single Track Scale indicates potentially challenging and varied surfaces. Beginners should be prepared for some difficult parts and not expect an easy, smooth ride throughout. It's best suited for riders with some mountain biking experience and good physical condition.

faqs.question: What is the terrain like on the Cerro Lopera mountain bike trail?
faqs.answer: The terrain is diverse and can be quite demanding. You'll encounter an impressive mountain massif with an underfoot surface that is 'quite rough in places'. Technical difficulty ranges from S3 to S6, meaning you can expect varied surfaces that may include loose rocks, roots, steep sections, and potentially some areas where pushing your bike might be necessary. The varied geology, including dolomitic marbles and limestone, adds to the dramatic landscape.

faqs.question: What kind of views can I expect along the Cerro Lopera mountain bike route?
faqs.answer: You can expect 'stunning views' throughout the ride. The route offers panoramic vistas of the Sierras de Alhama, Tejeda y Almijara Natural Park, and even the more distant Sierra Nevada. The landscape is particularly beautiful in winter and spring when the surrounding peaks are capped with snow. You'll find 'fabulous views' from climbs, such as the one from Mesón Los Prados.

faqs.question: When is the best time of year to mountain bike the Cerro Lopera trail?
faqs.answer: The landscape is particularly beautiful in winter and spring when the surrounding peaks are capped with snow, offering a unique riding experience. Generally, spring and autumn provide the most pleasant temperatures for mountain biking in this region, avoiding the intense heat of summer. Winter can be great, but be prepared for colder conditions and potential snow at higher elevations.

faqs.question: Are there any notable landmarks or viewpoints along the Cerro Lopera mountain bike trail?
faqs.answer: Yes, a key highlight is the Cerro Lopera Summit and Lookout, which offers exceptional panoramic views. You'll also pass by Mesón Los Prados, which is a restaurant and a notable point on the route. Beyond these specific highlights, the entire trail is within the protected Sierras de Tejeda, Almijara y Alhama Natural Park, providing continuous dramatic mountain scenery.

faqs.question: Does the Cerro Lopera mountain bike route connect with other trails in the Natural Park?
faqs.answer: Yes, this route intersects with several other trails. You'll find sections that overlap with the Transandalus, Andalucía - South Route (Provincia de Granada), Sendero Pico de Lopera, and Camino de Jayena a los Prados, among others. This offers opportunities to explore further or combine sections with other known paths in the region.

faqs.question: Are dogs allowed on the Cerro Lopera mountain bike trail?
faqs.answer: Information regarding specific dog rules for this exact trail is not provided. However, as the trail is located within the Sierras de Tejeda, Almijara y Alhama Natural Park, it's generally advisable to keep dogs on a leash to protect wildlife and ensure a pleasant experience for all visitors. Always check the latest park regulations before your visit.

faqs.question: Do I need any special permits or pay an entrance fee to mountain bike in the Sierras de Tejeda, Almijara y Alhama Natural Park?
faqs.answer: Generally, there are no entrance fees to access the Sierras de Tejeda, Almijara y Alhama Natural Park for activities like mountain biking. However, specific regulations might apply to certain areas or for organized events. It's always a good idea to check the official website of the Natural Park for any updated rules or permit requirements, especially if you plan to ride in sensitive ecological zones.

faqs.question: Is there a recommended direction to ride the Cerro Lopera circular trail, or any specific sections to be aware of?
faqs.answer: While the route is circular, the description mentions 'fabulous views' from climbs such as the one from Mesón Los Prados. Given the S3-S6 technical sections and 642 meters of elevation gain, riding in a direction that allows you to tackle the steepest or most technical climbs when you're freshest, or to enjoy the best views on descents, could enhance your experience. Reviewing the Komoot tour profile for elevation changes will help you decide the best direction for your fitness level.
